job.title: Windows Protocol Expert / SME
job.description:
REDLattice is looking for engineers with security clearances. We are a dynamic company in the midst of amazing growth. Culture and employee happiness is our focus; which is why we sponsor many social events including game nights, CTF's, happy hours and outings. We offer top-notch benefits and employee ownership that makes offers from our company pretty sweet. In addition, REDLattice was voted one of Washingtonian's Top 50 Employers and Washington Business Journal's Top 100 Employers in 2019.  

We need talented folks who want to work on the forefront of cyber. We aren’t talking network defense or basic IT security here. Our positions are mission focused and operational in nature. As a REDLattice Engineer, projects will be undertaken in small teams with close coordination with customers to develop new or enhance existing tools. 

Required Qualifications

  • Research and Development opportunity to exercise deep understanding of Windows protocols and underlying networking protocols

  • Extensive experience with Analyzing data packets and writing wireshark dissectors

  • Extensive experience reverse engineering and analyzing network protocols

  • Extensive C and Python development experience

  • Knowledge of current protocol standards and implementations

  • An active Security Clearance

Preferable Experience:

  • Experience with virtualization and containerization (Python virtual environments and Docker)

  • CI/CD design/maintenance

  • Atlassian Suite (Jira, Confluence, Crucible)

  • Subversion exposure

REDLattice Inc. is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We consider applicants without regard to race, color, religion, age, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, veteran status, disability, genetic information, citizenship status, or membership in any other group protected by federal, state or local law.

Disclaimer:

This job description indicates in general the nature and levels of work, knowledge, skills, abilities and other essential functions (as covered under the Americans with Disabilities Act) expected of an incumbent.  It is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities required of an incumbent.  An incumbent may be asked to perform other duties as required.


·

job.title: Windows Kernel Developers
job.description:
REDLattice is looking for engineers with security clearances. We are a dynamic company in the midst of amazing growth. Culture and employee happiness is our focus; which is why we sponsor many social events including game nights, CTF's, happy hours and outings. We offer top-notch benefits and employee ownership that makes offers from our company pretty sweet. In addition, REDLattice was voted one of Washingtonian's Top 50 Employers and Washington Business Journal's Top 100 Employers in 2019.  

We need talented folks who want to work on the forefront of cyber. We aren’t talking network defense or basic IT security here. Our positions are mission focused and operational in nature. As a REDLattice Engineer, projects will be undertaken in small teams with close coordination with customers to develop new or enhance existing tools. 

Required Qualifications

  • Research and development experience with creating windows kernel drivers

  • Extensive experience with WinDBG and Visual Studio

  • Strong C and Python development experience

  • Demonstrated ability to self-drive research tasking

  • Motivated to learn complex systems

  • Will work with a team of seasoned researchers and developers

  • An active Security Clearance

Preferable Experience:

  • Experience with virtualization and containerization (Python virtual environments and Docker)

  • CI/CD design/maintenance

  • Atlassian Suite (Jira, Confluence, Crucible)

  • Subversion exposure

REDLattice Inc. is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We consider applicants without regard to race, color, religion, age, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, veteran status, disability, genetic information, citizenship status, or membership in any other group protected by federal, state or local law.

Disclaimer:

This job description indicates in general the nature and levels of work, knowledge, skills, abilities and other essential functions (as covered under the Americans with Disabilities Act) expected of an incumbent.  It is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities required of an incumbent.  An incumbent may be asked to perform other duties as required.

job.title: Application Reverse Engineering Expert/SME
job.description:
REDLattice is looking for engineers with security clearances. We are a dynamic company in the midst of amazing growth. Culture and employee happiness is our focus; which is why we sponsor many social events including game nights, CTF's, happy hours and outings. We offer top-notch benefits and employee ownership that makes offers from our company pretty sweet. In addition, REDLattice was voted one of Washingtonian's Top 50 Employers and Washington Business Journal's Top 100 Employers in 2019.  

We need talented folks who want to work on the forefront of cyber. We aren’t talking network defense or basic IT security here. Our positions are mission focused and operational in nature. As an Android Developer, projects will be undertaken in small teams with close coordination with customers to develop new or enhance existing tools. 

Responsibilities

Help our customers to solve the hardest security problems
Work in small groups and independently to build mission critical tools
Perform technical research utilizing a variety of sources for evaluating both operational tools and processes

Required Qualifications

  • Research and Development opportunity to exercise deep reverse engineering skills to tear apart applications and document functionality

  • Requires C and Python development experience

  • Ability to write in-line Assembly preferred

  • Extensive experience with IDAPro/Ghidra/Binary Ninja and writing custom plug-ins to support analysis

  • This position requires the candidate to have a current security clearance

Preferable Experience:

  • Experience with virtualization and containerization (Python virtual environments and Docker)

  • CI/CD design/maintenance

  • Atlassian Suite (Jira, Confluence, Crucible)

  • Subversion exposure

REDLattice Inc. is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We consider applicants without regard to race, color, religion, age, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, veteran status, disability, genetic information, citizenship status, or membership in any other group protected by federal, state or local law.

Disclaimer:

This job description indicates in general the nature and levels of work, knowledge, skills, abilities and other essential functions (as covered under the Americans with Disabilities Act) expected of an incumbent.  It is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities required of an incumbent.  An incumbent may be asked to perform other duties as required.

job.title: CNO Developer
job.description:
REDLattice is looking for engineers with security clearances. We are a dynamic company in the midst of amazing growth. Culture and employee happiness is our focus; which is why we sponsor many social events including game nights, CTF's, happy hours and outings. We offer top-notch benefits and employee ownership that makes offers from our company pretty sweet. In addition, REDLattice was voted one of Washingtonian's Top 50 Employers and Washington Business Journal's Top 100 Employers in 2019.  

We need talented folks who want to work on the forefront of cyber. We aren’t talking network defense or basic IT security here. Our positions are mission focused and operational in nature. As an Android Developer, projects will be undertaken in small teams with close coordination with customers to develop new or enhance existing tools. 

Responsibilities

Help our customers to solve the hardest security problems
Work in small groups and independently to build mission critical tools
Perform technical research utilizing a variety of sources for evaluating both operational tools and processes

Required Qualifications

  • 3+ years of professional software development (specifically in Python, C/C++, JAVA)

  • Strong C/C++ development experience

  • Strong knowledge of operating systems internals

  • Strong Python development experience including ctypes and unittest

  • Adept and disciplined in test-driven development (TDD)

  • Willingness to improve complex technical design issues

  • Willingness to correct and update documentation

  • This position requires the candidate to have a current security clearance

Preferable Experience:

  • Experience with virtualization and containerization (Python virtual environments and Docker)

  • CI/CD design/maintenance

  • Atlassian Suite (Jira, Confluence, Crucible)

  • Subversion exposure

REDLattice Inc. is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We consider applicants without regard to race, color, religion, age, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, veteran status, disability, genetic information, citizenship status, or membership in any other group protected by federal, state or local law.

job.title: Embedded Systems Engineer
job.description:
REDLattice is looking for engineers with security clearances. We are a dynamic company in the midst of amazing growth. Culture and employee happiness is our focus; which is why we sponsor many social events including game nights, CTF's, happy hours and outings. We offer top-notch benefits and employee ownership that makes offers from our company pretty sweet. In addition, REDLattice was voted one of Washingtonian's Top 50 Employers and Washington Business Journal's Top 100 Employers in 2019.  

We need talented folks who want to work on the forefront of cyber. We aren’t talking network defense or basic IT security here. Our positions are mission focused and operational in nature. As a REDLattice Engineer, projects will be undertaken in small teams with close coordination with customers to develop new or enhance existing tools. 

Required Qualifications

  • 3+ years experience in developing on embedded platforms (Embedded Linux, ARM, low-power architectures) 
  • 3+ years experience using C/C++, Python 
  • Demonstrated experience performing Software Configuration Management 
  • Demonstrated ability to work in a dynamic and challenging environment 
  • Demonstrated in depth understanding of low level systems development for Linux internals and API 
  • An active Security Clearance

Desired Qualifications

  • Linux kernel and device driver programming 
  • Understanding of network cyber techniques to include application and mitigation 
  • Vulnerability Research

REDLattice Inc. is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We consider applicants without regard to race, color, religion, age, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, veteran status, disability, genetic information, citizenship status, or membership in any other group protected by federal, state or local law.

Disclaimer:

This job description indicates in general the nature and levels of work, knowledge, skills, abilities and other essential functions (as covered under the Americans with Disabilities Act) expected of an incumbent.  It is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities required of an incumbent.  An incumbent may be asked to perform other duties as required.

job.title: Vulnerability Engineer
job.description:
REDLattice is looking for engineers with security clearances. We are a dynamic company in the midst of amazing growth. Culture and employee happiness is our focus; which is why we sponsor many social events including game nights, CTF's, happy hours and outings. We offer top-notch benefits and employee ownership that makes offers from our company pretty sweet. 

We need talented folks who want to work on the forefront of cyber. We aren’t talking network defense or basic IT security here. Our positions are mission focused and operational in nature.  

 

Responsibilities

To provide software reverse engineering and vulnerability research expertise as part of a research team based in Melbourne, FL.

This role is focused on custom solutions that allow exploitation of mobile and embedded devices as part of a team developers, reverse engineers and exploitation specialists. The candidates will need to have experience of binary reverse engineering and software exploit discovery and exploitation.

 

Required Qualifications

  • Formal degree in Computer Science or Computer Engineering, or another related field.
  • Minimum of 5+ years in IT application development with strong understanding and experience in software security programming, and 2+ years in Vulnerability Research / Reverse Engineering and must be comfortable working in an extreme agile environment with frequent course changes.
  • Experience using IDA Pro to determine how an application works and processes data.  This could include x86, ARM, ARM64 etc.
  • Experience identifying and exploiting zero days including memory corruption bugs for example stack overflows, heap overflows, integer overflows, logical flaws. 
  • Experience with mitigation (ASLR, Stack cookies, non-executable memory) to deter exploitation and how can they be bypassed.
  • File format reverse engineering – Experience determining how files are structured, understanding the standard methods for encoding data from Base64 to ASN1.
  • Understand how symmetrical and asymmetrical encryption works, certificate chain of trust, crypto weaknesses etc.
  • Knowledge of how IP/Serial based protocols work and how to reverse their format including checksums, MACs, encoding formats, HTTP, XML etc.
  • Experience of writing and running fuzzers, understanding of the differences between dumb and more intelligent fuzzers, and how Reverse Engineering feeds the process.
  • Ability to quickly write programs to accomplish point solutions in languages like Python, C, C++, C#, PHP and java.
  • Ability to review C/C++ source code for vulnerabilities.
  • Understanding of how operating systems function from user interface code through to the kernel.
  • Experience programming Android applications and securing the operating system and applications.
  • Experience developing forensic tools to support mission requirements from the output the vulnerability research
  • Possession of an active US security clearance

 

REDLattice Inc. is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We consider applicants without regard to race, color, religion, age, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, veteran status, disability, genetic information, citizenship status, or membership in any other group protected by federal, state or local law.

 

Disclaimer:

This job description indicates in general the nature and levels of work, knowledge, skills, abilities and other essential functions (as covered under the Americans with Disabilities Act) expected of an incumbent.  It is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities required of an incumbent.  An incumbent may be asked to perform other duties as required.

job.title: Mobile Application Software Reverse Engineer
job.description:
REDLattice is looking for engineers with security clearances. We are a dynamic company in the midst of amazing growth. Culture and employee happiness is our focus; which is why we sponsor many social events including game nights, CTF's, happy hours and outings. We offer top-notch benefits and employee ownership that makes offers from our company pretty sweet. 

We need talented folks who want to work on the forefront of cyber. We aren’t talking network defense or basic IT security here. Our positions are mission focused and operational in nature.  

 

Responsibilities

To provide software reverse engineering and vulnerability research expertise as part of a research team based in Melbourne, FL.

This role is focused on custom solutions that allow exploitation of mobile and embedded devices as part of a team developers, reverse engineers and exploitation specialists. The candidates will need to do research utilizing a variety of sources for evaluating both operational tools and processes. Candidate shall provide on-site support in research and development designed to exploit vulnerabilities of adversary technology. Candidates will analyze and reverse engineer source code and binaries of various software technologies. Finally, candidates will perform software integration, testing, scripting and development of algorithms and support tools.

 

Required Qualifications

  • Formal degree in Computer Science or Computer Engineering, or another related field.
  • Minimum of 5+ years in IT application development with strong understanding and experience in software security programming, and 2+ years in Vulnerability Research / Reverse Engineering and must be comfortable working in an extreme agile environment with frequent course changes.
  • Knowledge of and experience with binary reverse engineering and software vulnerability discovery
  • Experience developing, debugging and / or reverse engineering code for popular mobile programming languages (i.e. Java, Objective-C, Swift, etc)
  • Experience with mobile application reverse engineering tools (i.e. JEB, Hopper, IDA)
  • Experience determining how files are structured, understanding the standard methods for encoding data from Base64 to ASN1.
  • Comfortable viewing, analyzing and understanding raw binary data
  • Experience with relational database management systems (i.e. SQL and SQLite)
  • Ability to quickly write programs to accomplish point solutions in a variety of languages, such as C, C++, Python, PHP, Objective-C and Java
  • Experience working in an agile development environment with short duration tasking.
  • Ability to analyze and decode data packets over a networked connection, and experience with network analysis tools (e.g. Wireshark).
  • Protocols – knowledge of how IP/Serial based protocols work and how to reverse their format including checksums, MACs, encoding formats, HTTP, XML etc.
  • Cryptoanalysis – possess an understanding of how symmetrical and asymmetrical encryption functions, certificate chain of trust, crypto weaknesses, etc.
  • Possession of a US security clearance.

 

REDLattice Inc. is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We consider applicants without regard to race, color, religion, age, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, veteran status, disability, genetic information, citizenship status, or membership in any other group protected by federal, state or local law.

 

Disclaimer:

This job description indicates in general the nature and levels of work, knowledge, skills, abilities and other essential functions (as covered under the Americans with Disabilities Act) expected of an incumbent.  It is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities required of an incumbent.  An incumbent may be asked to perform other duties as required.

job.title: iOS Developer
job.description:
REDLattice is looking for engineers with security clearances. We are a dynamic company in the midst of amazing growth. Culture and employee happiness is our focus; which is why we sponsor many social events including game nights, CTF's, happy hours and outings. We offer top-notch benefits and employee ownership that makes offers from our company pretty sweet. 

We need talented folks who want to work on the forefront of cyber. We aren’t talking network defense or basic IT security here. Our positions are mission focused and operational in nature. As a REDLattice Engineer, projects will be undertaken in small teams with close coordination with customers to develop new or enhance existing tools. 

 

Required Qualifications

  • 3+ years of expertise in software development - specifically Python, C/C++, ARM Assembly
  • 3+ years of experience programming offensive tools and techniques
  • Understanding of network cyber techniques to include application and mitigation
  • 6 months+ of applied knowledge/experience developing and testing applications on Mac, Apple OS X and/or iOS environments
  • Possess an intimate knowledge of designing and developing mobile end-to-end software solutions
  • Experience writing applications using iOS Software Development Kit (SDK)
  • Possession of a Security Clearance

 

Desired Qualifications

  • Demonstrated understanding of low level systems development for Linux, Android and Windows OS
  • Demonstrated experience in writing software in: Bash or JAVA
  • Demonstrated  experience performing Software Configuration Management

 

 

REDLattice Inc. is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We consider applicants without regard to race, color, religion, age, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, veteran status, disability, genetic information, citizenship status, or membership in any other group protected by federal, state or local law.

 

Disclaimer:

This job description indicates in general the nature and levels of work, knowledge, skills, abilities and other essential functions (as covered under the Americans with Disabilities Act) expected of an incumbent.  It is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities required of an incumbent.  An incumbent may be asked to perform other duties as required.

job.title: Android Developer
job.description:
REDLattice is looking for engineers with security clearances. We are a dynamic company in the midst of amazing growth. Culture and employee happiness is our focus; which is why we sponsor many social events including game nights, CTF's, happy hours and outings. We offer top-notch benefits and employee ownership that makes offers from our company pretty sweet. 

We need talented folks who want to work on the forefront of cyber. We aren’t talking network defense or basic IT security here. Our positions are mission focused and operational in nature. As an Android Developer, projects will be undertaken in small teams with close coordination with customers to develop new or enhance existing tools. 

 

Responsibilities

Help our customers to design custom software tools and techniques on mobile platforms

Work in small groups and independently to build mission critical tools

Perform technical research utilizing a variety of sources for evaluating both operational tools and processes

 

Required Qualifications

  • This position requires the candidate to have a current US security clearance
  • 3+ years of professional software development (specifically in C/C++, ARM Assembly, and Python)
  • 3+ years of mobile development, specifically Android development (Android Studio)
  • 3+ years experience Reverse Engineering
  • Possess an intimate knowledge of the Android Operating System and internals
  • Understanding of network cyber techniques to include application and mitigation

Desired Qualifications

  • 3-5 years of experience in programming security or systems
  • Experience with advanced debugging and reverse engineering tools
  • 3+ years of knowledge/experience in developing on Linux
  • Vulnerability Research
  • Understanding public exploits and awareness of new unpublished exploits
  • Understanding and knowledge of security evasion techniques

 

REDLattice Inc. is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We consider applicants without regard to race, color, religion, age, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, veteran status, disability, genetic information, citizenship status, or membership in any other group protected by federal, state or local law.

 

Disclaimer:

This job description indicates in general the nature and levels of work, knowledge, skills, abilities and other essential functions (as covered under the Americans with Disabilities Act) expected of an incumbent.  It is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities required of an incumbent.  An incumbent may be asked to perform other duties as required.

job.title: Android CNO Developer
job.description:
REDLattice is looking for engineers with security clearances. We are a dynamic company in the midst of amazing growth. Culture and employee happiness is our focus; which is why we sponsor many social events including game nights, CTF's, happy hours and outings. We offer top-notch benefits and employee ownership that makes offers from our company pretty sweet. In addition, REDLattice was voted one of Washingtonian's Top 50 Employers and Washington Business Journal's Top 100 Employers in 2019.  

We need talented folks who want to work on the forefront of cyber. We aren’t talking network defense or basic IT security here. Our positions are mission focused and operational in nature. As an Android Developer, projects will be undertaken in small teams with close coordination with customers to develop new or enhance existing tools. 

 

Responsibilities

  • Help our customers to solve the hardest security problems
  • Work in small groups and independently to build mission critical tools
  • Perform technical research utilizing a variety of sources for evaluating both operational tools and processes
  • Work/enhance existing frameworks and operational tools to support ongoing missions
  • Research, design and develop new capabilities based on mission need and security enhancements

 

Required Qualifications

  • 3+ years of professional software development (specifically in Python, C/C++, JAVA)
  • 3+ years of Android development (Android Studio)
  • Possess an intimate knowledge of the Android Operating System and internals
  • Understanding of offensive network cyber techniques to include application and mitigation
  • This position requires the candidate to have a current security clearance. 

 

Desired Qualifications

  • 3-5 years of experience in programming security or systems
  • Knowledge/experience in developing on Linux
  • Experience with advanced debugging and reverse engineering tools
  • Knowledge/experience in developing on iOS
  • Understanding public exploits and awareness of new unpublished exploits
  • Understanding and knowledge of security evasion techniques
  • This position requires the candidate to have a current security clearance. 

 

REDLattice Inc. is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We consider applicants without regard to race, color, religion, age, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, veteran status, disability, genetic information, citizenship status, or membership in any other group protected by federal, state or local law.

job.title: Windows CNO Tool Developer
job.description:
REDLattice is looking for engineers with security clearances. We are a dynamic company in the midst of amazing growth. Culture and employee happiness is our focus; which is why we sponsor many social events including game nights, CTF's, happy hours and outings. We offer top-notch benefits and employee ownership that makes offers from our company pretty sweet. In addition, REDLattice was voted one of Washingtonian's Top 50 Employers and Washington Business Journal's Top 100 Employers in 2019.  

We need talented folks who want to work on the forefront of cyber. We aren’t talking network defense or basic IT security here. Our positions are mission focused and operational in nature. As a REDLattice Engineer, projects will be undertaken in small teams with close coordination with customers to develop new or enhance existing tools. 

Responsibilities

Help our customers to solve the hardest security problems
Work in small groups and independently to build mission critical tools
Perform technical research utilizing a variety of sources for evaluating both operational tools and processes

Required Qualifications

  • Experience in C/C++ 
  • Experience with Windows Internals/APIs
  • Possess an intimate knowledge of the Windows Operating System and Windows programming (user mode/kernel mode)
  • Knowledge and experience using industry standard encryption techniques and network protocols
  • Understanding of technical issues surrounding the designing, building and testing of software development kits supporting platforms and frameworks
  • Applied knowledge/experience developing and testing applications on Windows systems
  • Experience with advanced debugging and low-level debugging using Visual Studio and/or other products
  • Understanding and knowledge using agile development practices
  • Security Clearance

Desired Qualifications

  • Familiarity with Apache, Git, and Atlassian products
  • Experience with user-level linux (bash scripting, etc.)
  • Familiarity and experience with reverse engineering kernel level API calls
  • Experience with kernel level debugging using WinDbg and Visual Studio
  • Linux and Macintosh (32 & 64 bit) Application and Internals experience
  • Applied knowledge/experience developing and testing applications on Linux and Macintosh systems

 

REDLattice Inc. is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We consider applicants without regard to race, color, religion, age, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, veteran status, disability, genetic information, citizenship status, or membership in any other group protected by federal, state or local law.

 

Disclaimer:

This job description indicates in general the nature and levels of work, knowledge, skills, abilities and other essential functions (as covered under the Americans with Disabilities Act) expected of an incumbent.  It is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities required of an incumbent.  An incumbent may be asked to perform other duties as required.

job.description:
REDLattice is looking for engineers with security clearances. We are a dynamic company in the midst of amazing growth. Culture and employee happiness is our focus; which is why we sponsor many social events including game nights, CTF's, happy hours and outings. We offer top-notch benefits and employee ownership that makes offers from our company pretty sweet. 

We need talented folks who want to work on the forefront of cyber. We aren’t talking network defense or basic IT security here. Our positions are mission focused and operational in nature. As an Android Developer, projects will be undertaken in small teams with close coordination with customers to develop new or enhance existing tools. 

 

Responsibilities

Help our customers to design custom software tools and techniques on mobile platforms

Work in small groups and independently to build mission critical tools

Perform technical research utilizing a variety of sources for evaluating both operational tools and processes

 

Required Qualifications

  • This position requires the candidate to have a current US security clearance
  • 3+ years of professional software development (specifically in C/C++ and Python)
  • 3+ years of Android development (Android Studio)
  • 3+ years experience Reverse Engineering
  • Possess an intimate knowledge of the Android Operating System and internals

  • Experience with advanced debugging and reverse engineering tools
  • 3+ years of knowledge/experience in developing on Linux
  • ARM assembly
  • Vulnerability Research

job.title: CNO Developer
job.description:
REDLattice is looking for engineers with security clearances. We are a dynamic company and we are going through amazing growth right now. We offer top-notch benefits and employee ownership that makes offers from our company pretty sweet.

We need talented folks who want to work on the forefront of Computer Network Operations. We aren’t talking network defense or basic IT security here. Our positions are mission focused and operational in nature. As a CNO Developer, projects will be undertaken in small teams with close coordination with customers to quickly enhance capabilities or resolve issues in existing tools.

Required Qualifications

  • 3+ years experience with application and kernel development on Windows, Linux, Mobile, Embedded Systems
  • 3+ years experience using C/C++, x86/X64 assembly, Python, or other scripting languages
  • 3+ years experience reverse engineering (IDA Pro, GHIDRA0, WinDdb, etc.)
  • CNO background
  • Experience with software development processes and lifecycles to include agile development
  • Network Communications development
  • Security Clearance

Desired Qualifications

  • Vulnerability Research
  • Mobile / Embedded Development
  • Experience developing covert applications, network security, and encryption
  • Familiarity with modern exploit mitigation techniques and counter-measures

 

REDLattice Inc. is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We consider applicants without regard to race, color, religion, age, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, veteran status, disability, genetic information, citizenship status, or membership in any other group protected by federal, state or local law.

 

Disclaimer:

This job description indicates in general the nature and levels of work, knowledge, skills, abilities and other essential functions (as covered under the Americans with Disabilities Act) expected of an incumbent.  It is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities required of an incumbent.  An incumbent may be asked to perform other duties as required.

job.title: iOS Engineer
job.description:
REDLattice is looking for engineers with security clearances. We are a dynamic company in the midst of amazing growth. Culture and employee happiness is our focus; which is why we sponsor many social events including game nights, CTF's, happy hours and outings. We offer top-notch benefits and employee ownership that makes offers from our company pretty sweet. 

We need talented folks who want to work on the forefront of cyber. We aren’t talking network defense or basic IT security here. Our positions are mission focused and operational in nature. As a REDLattice Engineer, projects will be undertaken in small teams with close coordination with customers to develop new or enhance existing tools. 

 

Required Qualifications

  • 2+ years of expertise in software development - specifically Python, C/C++, Objective-C
  • 2+ years of experience programming offensive tools and techniques
  • Understanding of network cyber techniques to include application and mitigation
  • 6 months+ of applied knowledge/experience developing and testing applications on Mac, Apple OS X and/or iOS environments
  • Possess an intimate knowledge of designing and developing mobile end-to-end software solutions
  • Experience writing applications using iOS Software Development Kit (SDK)
  • Possession of a Security Clearance

 

Desired Qualifications

  • Demonstrated understanding of low level systems development for Linux, Android and Windows OS
  • Demonstrated experience in writing software in: Bash or JAVA
  • Demonstrated  experience performing Software Configuration Management

 

 

REDLattice Inc. is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We consider applicants without regard to race, color, religion, age, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, veteran status, disability, genetic information, citizenship status, or membership in any other group protected by federal, state or local law.

 

Disclaimer:

This job description indicates in general the nature and levels of work, knowledge, skills, abilities and other essential functions (as covered under the Americans with Disabilities Act) expected of an incumbent.  It is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities required of an incumbent.  An incumbent may be asked to perform other duties as required.

job.title: iOS Engineer
job.description:
REDLattice is looking for engineers with security clearances. We are a dynamic company in the midst of amazing growth. Culture and employee happiness is our focus; which is why we sponsor many social events including game nights, CTF's, happy hours and outings. We offer top-notch benefits and employee ownership that makes offers from our company pretty sweet. 

We need talented folks who want to work on the forefront of cyber. We aren’t talking network defense or basic IT security here. Our positions are mission focused and operational in nature. As a REDLattice Engineer, projects will be undertaken in small teams with close coordination with customers to develop new or enhance existing tools. 

 

Required Qualifications

  • 2+ years of expertise in software development - specifically Python, C/C++, Objective-C
  • 2+ years of experience programming offensive tools and techniques
  • Understanding of network cyber techniques to include application and mitigation
  • 6 months+ of applied knowledge/experience developing and testing applications on Mac, Apple OS X and/or iOS environments
  • Possess an intimate knowledge of designing and developing mobile end-to-end software solutions
  • Experience writing applications using iOS Software Development Kit (SDK)
  • Possession of a Security Clearance

 

Desired Qualifications

  • Demonstrated understanding of low level systems development for Linux, Android and Windows OS
  • Demonstrated experience in writing software in: Bash or JAVA
  • Demonstrated  experience performing Software Configuration Management

 

 

REDLattice Inc. is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We consider applicants without regard to race, color, religion, age, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, veteran status, disability, genetic information, citizenship status, or membership in any other group protected by federal, state or local law.

 

Disclaimer:

This job description indicates in general the nature and levels of work, knowledge, skills, abilities and other essential functions (as covered under the Americans with Disabilities Act) expected of an incumbent.  It is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities required of an incumbent.  An incumbent may be asked to perform other duties as required.

job.title: Linux CNO Tool Developer
job.description:
REDLattice is looking for engineers with security clearances. We are a dynamic company in the midst of amazing growth. Culture and employee happiness is our focus; which is why we sponsor many social events including game nights, CTF's, happy hours and outings. We offer top-notch benefits and employee ownership that makes offers from our company pretty sweet. In addition, REDLattice was voted one of Washingtonian's Top 50 Employers and Washington Business Journal's Top 100 Employers in 2019.  

We need talented folks who want to work on the forefront of cyber. We aren’t talking network defense or basic IT security here. Our positions are mission focused and operational in nature. As a REDLattice Engineer, projects will be undertaken in small teams with close coordination with customers to develop new or enhance existing tools. 

 

Responsibilities:

Help our customers to solve the hardest security problems
Work in small groups and independently to build mission critical tools
Perform technical research utilizing a variety of sources for evaluating both operational tools and processes

Required Qualifications:

  • 1+ years of experience in C/C++ 
  • 1+ years of experience with Python programming
  • 1+ years of experience with user-level Linux (bash scripting, etc.)
  • Demonstrated in depth understanding of low-level systems development for Linux kernel,  internals and APIs
  • Knowledge and experience using industry standard encryption techniques and network protocols
  • Understanding of technical issues surrounding the designing, building and testing of software development kits supporting platforms and frameworks
  • Applied knowledge/experience developing and testing applications on Linux systems
  • Understanding of network cyber techniques to include application and mitigation
  • Understanding and knowledge using agile development practices
  • Active and current security clearance

Desired Qualifications:

  • Familiarity with Apache, Git, and Atlassian products
  • Experience in developing on embedded platforms
  • Familiarity and experience with reverse engineering kernel level API calls
  • Experience with kernel level debugging
  • Mobile and Macintosh (32 & 64 bit) Application and Internals experience
  • Applied knowledge/experience developing and testing applications on mobile and Macintosh systems

 

REDLattice Inc. is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We consider applicants without regard to race, color, religion, age, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, veteran status, disability, genetic information, citizenship status, or membership in any other group protected by federal, state or local law.

 

Disclaimer:

This job description indicates in general the nature and levels of work, knowledge, skills, abilities and other essential functions (as covered under the Americans with Disabilities Act) expected of an incumbent.  It is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities required of an incumbent.  An incumbent may be asked to perform other duties as required.

job.title: Android Developer
job.description:
REDLattice is looking for engineers with security clearances. We are a dynamic company in the midst of amazing growth. Culture and employee happiness is our focus; which is why we sponsor many social events including game nights, CTF's, happy hours and outings. We offer top-notch benefits and employee ownership that makes offers from our company pretty sweet. In addition, REDLattice was voted one of Washingtonian's Top 50 Employers and Washington Business Journal's Top 100 Employers in 2019.  

We need talented folks who want to work on the forefront of cyber. We aren’t talking network defense or basic IT security here. Our positions are mission focused and operational in nature. As an Android Developer, projects will be undertaken in small teams with close coordination with customers to develop new or enhance existing tools. 

Responsibilities

Help our customers to solve the hardest security problems
Work in small groups and independently to build mission critical tools
Perform technical research utilizing a variety of sources for evaluating both operational tools and processes

Required Qualifications

  • 3+ years of professional software development (specifically in Python, C/C++, JAVA)
  • 3+ years of Android development (Android Studio)
  • Possess an intimate knowledge of the Android Operating System and internals
  • Understanding of network cyber techniques to include application and mitigation
  • This position requires the candidate to have a current security clearance

Desired Qualifications

  • 3-5 years of experience in programming security or systems
  • 3+ years of knowledge/experience in developing on Linux
  • Experience with advanced debugging and reverse engineering tools
  • Understanding public exploits and awareness of new unpublished exploits
  • Understanding and knowledge of security evasion techniques

 

REDLattice Inc. is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We consider applicants without regard to race, color, religion, age, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, veteran status, disability, genetic information, citizenship status, or membership in any other group protected by federal, state or local law.

 

Disclaimer:

This job description indicates in general the nature and levels of work, knowledge, skills, abilities and other essential functions (as covered under the Americans with Disabilities Act) expected of an incumbent.  It is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities required of an incumbent.  An incumbent may be asked to perform other duties as required.

job.title: CNO Software Engineer
job.description:
REDLattice is looking for engineers with security clearances. We are a dynamic company in the midst of amazing growth. Culture and employee happiness is our focus; which is why we sponsor many social events including game nights, CTF's, happy hours and outings. We offer top-notch benefits and employee ownership that makes offers from our company pretty sweet. In addition, REDLattice was voted one of Washingtonian's Top 50 Employers and Washington Business Journal's Top 100 Employers in 2019.  

We need talented folks who want to work on the forefront of cyber. We aren’t talking network defense or basic IT security here. Our positions are mission focused and operational in nature. As a REDLattice Engineer, projects will be undertaken in small teams with close coordination with customers to develop new or enhance existing tools. 

 

Responsibilities:

Help our customers to solve the hardest security problems
Work in small groups and independently to build mission critical tools
Perform technical research utilizing a variety of sources for evaluating both operational tools and processes

Required Qualifications:

  • Active and current security clearance
  • Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science or related field, or equivalent experience
  • 5+ years of experience in C/C++ 
  • 5+ years of experience with Python programming
  • Demonstrated in depth understanding of low-level systems development, kernel, internals and APIs
  • Knowledge and experience using industry standard encryption techniques and network protocols
  • Understanding of technical issues surrounding the designing, building and testing of software development kits supporting platforms and frameworks
  • Applied knowledge/experience developing and testing applications on Linux systems
  • Understanding of network cyber techniques to include application and mitigation
  • Understanding and knowledge using agile development practices

Desired Qualifications:

  • Master's Degree in Computer Science or related field, or equivalent experience
  • Experience developing CNO tools
  • Familiarity and experience with reverse engineering kernel level API calls
  • Experience with kernel level debugging

 

REDLattice Inc. is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We consider applicants without regard to race, color, religion, age, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, veteran status, disability, genetic information, citizenship status, or membership in any other group protected by federal, state or local law.

 

Disclaimer:

job.title: Windows Software Engineer
job.description:
REDLattice is looking for engineers with security clearances. We are a dynamic company in the midst of amazing growth. Culture and employee happiness is our focus; which is why we sponsor many social events including game nights, CTF's, happy hours and outings. We offer top-notch benefits and employee ownership that makes offers from our company pretty sweet. In addition, REDLattice was voted one of Washingtonian's Top 50 Employers and Washington Business Journal's Top 100 Employers in 2019.  

We need talented folks who want to work on the forefront of cyber. We aren’t talking network defense or basic IT security here. Our positions are mission focused and operational in nature. As a REDLattice Engineer, projects will be undertaken in small teams with close coordination with customers to develop new or enhance existing tools. 

Responsibilities

Help our customers to solve the hardest security problems
Work in small groups and independently to build mission critical tools
Perform technical research utilizing a variety of sources for evaluating both operational tools and processes

Required Qualifications

  • Active Security Clearance
  • Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science or related field, or equivalent experience
  • Experience in C/C++ 
  • Experience with Windows Internals/APIs
  • Possess an intimate knowledge of the Windows Operating System and Windows programming (user mode/kernel mode)
  • Knowledge and experience using industry standard encryption techniques and network protocols
  • Understanding of technical issues surrounding the designing, building and testing of software development kits supporting platforms and frameworks
  • Applied knowledge/experience developing and testing applications on Windows systems
  • Applied knowledge/experience in PowerShell functionality
  • Experience with advanced debugging and low-level debugging using Visual Studio and/or other products
  • Understanding and knowledge using agile development practices

Desired Qualifications

  • Master's Degree in Computer Science or related field, or equivalent experience
  • Familiarity with Apache, Git, and Atlassian products
  • Experience with user-level linux (bash scripting, etc.)
  • Familiarity and experience with reverse engineering kernel level API calls
  • Experience with kernel level debugging using WinDbg and Visual Studio
  • Linux and Macintosh (32 & 64 bit) Application and Internals experience
  • Applied knowledge/experience developing and testing applications on Linux and Macintosh systems

 

REDLattice Inc. is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We consider applicants without regard to race, color, religion, age, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, veteran status, disability, genetic information, citizenship status, or membership in any other group protected by federal, state or local law.

Disclaimer:

This job description indicates in general the nature and levels of work, knowledge, skills, abilities and other essential functions (as covered under the Americans with Disabilities Act) expected of an incumbent.  It is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities required of an incumbent.  An incumbent may be asked to perform other duties as required.
